Mykhailo Fedorov has congratulated Mykhailo Drapatyi on his appointment and gave credit to the Ukrainian people for influencing Zelenskyy's decision to choose him.

"This is a breath of fresh air and new hope in the struggle... It is the voice of change that could not go unheard," he said in a statement.

"Now the high expectations of the Ukrainian people must be met with a clear vision for bringing the war of independence to an end, a strong team, and decisive actions aimed at preserving the lives of our soldiers, robotising the front line, delivering asymmetric strikes, and exhausting the Russian economy."

result.text:
Mykhailo Drapatyi was the commander of the legendary BMP-2 that broke through Russian-backed barricades in Mariupol on May 9, 2014.

At the time, Russian-backed militants were attempting to seize control of the city. Drapatyi led the armored breakthrough that helped Ukrainian forces regain the initiative and prevent Mariupol from falling. The footage became one of the defining symbols of Ukraine’s resistance in the early stages of the war.

result.text:
Zelensky dismisses Gen Syrsky and names Gen Mykhailo Drapaty as new commander in chief. 
Protests worked. Many in Ukraine rejoice at the choice. Drapaty, a hero of fighting for Mariupol back in 2014, is a favorite of many reformers. 
Zelensky said he also offered a new job in the security sector to former defense minister Fedorov. No word on whether Fedorov accepted.
Ukraine once again shows that it’s a free country where leaders have to listen to public opinion.

result.text:
Well, the ability to unite in a dark hour, take matters into our own hands, go out into the streets, and speak out against those in power has more than once saved our people when they stood on the brink of destruction.

Today, the fact that Ukraine remains a democracy despite everything has finally placed its best and most decent battlefield general, Mykhailo Drapatyi, at the head of its armed forces in this terrible war—a man supported almost unanimously by both the people and the army.

This is a great victory, one of the very many hard-won steps toward the light amid the darkness that have already been taken, with many more still ahead.

Today, the entire free world has something to celebrate.

data.translation:
A gigantic Russian factory for guided aerial bombs and warheads has reportedly been completely destroyed. Thousands of tons of explosives detonated—an industrial area that has in practice vanished from the map.  
This is not "damage." It is a strategic hole right in the heart of Russia's weapons production: KAB bombs, modernized FAB bombs, warheads for ballistic and cruise missiles. All gone after a series of massive explosions.

The Kremlin can replace soldiers. But replacing an entire weapons factory? That takes years—and Ukraine is counting the days.

result.text:
Discussions between former German and Russian officials regarding the war in Ukraine took place in Baku.

Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev confirmed that an informal, secret meeting of former officials was held in Baku from July 12 to 14. Nothing is known so far about the outcome.

The fact that former high-ranking German politicians are meeting with Russian representatives in Baku for secret talks would in itself require an explanation. The fact that there is subsequently a complete lack of transparency regarding the content, objectives, or outcomes is politically irresponsible.

Anyone utilizing unofficial channels during Russia’s war of aggression bears a special responsibility toward the public. Silence breeds speculation, mistrust, and disinformation—thereby playing right into the hands of the Kremlin, which has been attempting to divide German society for years.

If these talks were legitimate, the participants should disclose what was discussed and on whose behalf they were acting. 

Democracy thrives on transparency—not secret meetings.
